none: Wilkes-Barre urban renewal project : environmental impact statement.

The Wilkes-Barre Downtown Urban Renewal Project aims to rebuild the commercial center of Wilkes-Barre, Pennsylvania, which was severely damaged by Tropical Storm Agnes in 1972. The project covers 207.73 acres and involves removing flood-damaged structures, improving public facilities, and enhancing traffic flow and open spaces. The goal is to revitalize the area, improve living conditions, and boost the local economy. While the project promises environmental benefits like better air and water quality, it may negatively impact small businesses due to increased overhead costs during and after construction.
Wilkes-Barre urban renewal project : environmental impact statement.

The Wilkes-Barre Downtown Urban Renewal Project aims to rebuild the commercial center of Wilkes-Barre, Pennsylvania, which was severely damaged by Tropical Storm Agnes in 1972. The project covers 207.73 acres and involves removing flood-damaged structures, improving public facilities, and enhancing traffic flow and open spaces. The goal is to revitalize the area, improve living conditions, and boost the local economy. While the project promises environmental benefits like better air and water quality, it may negatively impact small businesses due to increased overhead costs during and after construction.
Department of Housing and Urban Development
Redevelopment Authority of the City of Wilkes-Barre

The Wilkes-Barre urban renewal project was proposed in response to the extensive damage caused by Tropical Storm Agnes in 1972, which devastated the commercial center of the city. There was some public concern regarding the potential negative impact on small businesses, as the revitalization efforts could lead to increased costs during construction. Ultimately, the project aimed to enhance the area and improve the local economy, but it faced challenges in balancing development with the needs of existing businesses.
United States. Department of Housing and Urban Development
United States. Department of Housing and Urban Development

"The Redevelopment Authority of the City of Wilkes-Barre is keenly aware of the small businessmen's problems and has the experience and determination to carry out its responsibilities toward their survival through the period of redevelopment and into the future." — Section 4.0 – Unavoidable Adverse Effects
